title: rhynchocorys elephas subsp. boissieri
intro: Rhynchocorys elephas subsp. boissieri, also known as the Boissier's Rhynchocorys, is a plant species that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rhynchocorys elephas subsp. boissieri include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and soothe irritated mucous membranes, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the leaves and flowers, which are then used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, allowing for the plant's medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
